text: HTTPS encrypts all data between a user's browser and a website, preventing hackers from reading sensitive information. Strong authentication systems, like multi-factor login, verify that users are who they claim to be. Input validation is also crucial: it checks user-submitted data against expected formats, blocking malicious code or SQL injection attempts. Common vulnerabilities include cross-site scripting and insecure direct object references. Developers must sanitize inputs, enforce secure session management, and regularly update software. By addressing these areas, websites become far more resistant to attacks and protect users' personal data effectively.
